codeReview.summary: The structural split is substantial and mostly coherent, but the deployment SSE stream behavior regressed in a way that breaks existing consumers.

issues.description: SSE event contract changed from "deployment"/"logs" to "state"/"log" and logs are emitted per-row instead of as a batch; existing dashboard client still listens for "deployment" and "logs" (web/dashboard/src/lib/api.ts:811,819), so live deployment updates/log streaming will not be processed.
New log polling is gated by `lastLogTS.Valid`, but `lastLogTS` starts invalid when there are no initial logs (line 310), so deployments that start with zero logs will never stream subsequent logs.
Initial `BuildLogsByBuildID` DB error is silently discarded (`logs, _ = ...`), which weakens observability and violates mission guidance against ignoring DB errors.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The refactor-worker procedure does not explicitly require API contract checks against existing route consumers after package splits, which likely contributed to the unnoticed SSE event-schema regression.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md focuses on build/vet/test and structural checks; regression appears at internal/rpc/deployments/handlers.go:297-338 while consumer expectations remain in web/dashboard/src/lib/api.ts:811-827.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture knowledge is now stale for RPC structure after this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md still states `internal/rpc` is a single package with "39 files, single API struct", but commit 3913da73 introduces domain sub-packages (auth/projects/deployments/volumes/domains/servers/settings).

summary: Review failed: the split achieved package decomposition targets, but deployment SSE behavior changed incompatibly (event names/payload contract) and introduced a log-streaming edge-case that prevents new logs from arriving when initial logs are empty.
